events.title: Disease in Congo 06/26/2026
events.body:
An Ebola Bundibugyo virus disease outbreak is ongoing across the Democratic Republic of the Congo and Uganda, according to a World Health Organization situation report covering data through June 21, 2026. Ebola Bundibugyo is a strain of the Ebola virus that causes hemorrhagic fever and can spread through direct contact with the blood or bodily fluids of an infected person.

Health authorities in both countries are actively monitoring the situation. The WHO Regional Office for Africa has been issuing weekly external situation reports, indicating that the outbreak response remains ongoing and under close international watch.

Residents in affected areas face risk of exposure, and health teams are working to contain the spread. People in these regions, including those in remote or underserved communities, may have limited access to treatment and support during the response.

Ashfall warnings are uncommon even during active volcanic periods and indicate that ash and debris are being carried by wind in amounts that could affect communities near the volcano. Residents in affected areas were advised to take precautions to protect their health and property from falling ash.

Kilauea has been one of the world's most continuously active volcanoes, but the frequency of eruptions in this current cycle and the issuance of an ashfall warning signal an ongoing period of heightened activity that continues to affect life for residents and visitors across the region.

events.title: Heatwave in Germany 06/27/2026
events.body:
On June 27, 2026, a heatwave swept across Western Europe, pushing temperatures more than 18°C above seasonal averages and setting all-time national records in several countries. Germany reached 41.5°C at a station in eastern Germany, its highest temperature ever recorded. The Czech Republic, Denmark, and the United Kingdom also broke national or all-time June records within the same weekend.

France bore the heaviest human toll, with deaths reported among both the young and elderly. Italy placed 18 cities — including Rome, Milan, and Venice — under red heat alerts. Across the region, hospitals struggled, rail services were disrupted, and roads and train tracks buckled under the heat.

Authorities in Germany urged residents to conserve water, and the national rail operator allowed passengers to cancel travel without charge. Heavy thunderstorms were expected to bring some relief by Sunday.
events.source_title: Al Jazeera – Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera
events.prayer: Families across Western Europe, including the young and elderly, enduring record-breaking heat
events.alert_summary: A heatwave pushed Germany to a national record of 41.5°C, with deaths reported across Western Europe, particularly in France.

events.title: Earthquake in Venezuela 06/27/2026 — Magnitude 8
events.body:
Two earthquakes struck northern Venezuela within seconds of each other on a Wednesday, with the second reaching a magnitude of 7.5 — one of the strongest to hit the country in a century. The region of La Guaira, north of the capital Caracas, suffered the worst damage, with hundreds of buildings destroyed or damaged, including hospitals and shopping centres. At least 172 people are still believed trapped under rubble.

The death toll stood at 920 as of Friday, with 3,360 injured. Surviving medical facilities are overwhelmed, and doctors say shortages of medicine and supplies made patient care difficult even before the disaster. There have been 214 aftershocks since the initial quakes.

Nearly 2,000 international rescue workers have joined the response, including 39 UN search and rescue teams equipped with dogs and small drones. At least 243 people have been pulled alive from rubble in La Guaira alone. Officials warn the death toll is likely to rise as search efforts continue.
events.prayer: Families in northern Venezuela searching for loved ones buried under earthquake rubble
events.alert_summary: Two earthquakes, the larger at magnitude 7.5, killed 920 people and injured 3,360 across northern Venezuela.

events.title: Flood in South Africa 06/26/2026
events.body:
Floods across southern Africa have killed or left missing hundreds of people, according to reports from the region. The full scale of the disaster is still emerging, with communities in multiple areas affected by rising and fast-moving waters.

Search and rescue efforts are underway as officials and emergency workers work to reach those in affected areas. Flooding of this kind can devastate homes, cut off roads, and disrupt access to clean water and food for weeks.

Residents in low-lying and rural communities are often among the hardest hit, with limited resources to recover quickly. Aid organizations are expected to respond as the situation becomes clearer in the coming days.
events.source_title: "flood OR flooding deaths OR displaced OR evacuated when:3d" - Google News
events.prayer: Families across southern Africa facing loss of life and missing loved ones amid floods
events.alert_summary: Floods across southern Africa have left hundreds of people killed or missing, no further figures reported.

events.title: Heatwave in Germany 06/26/2026
events.body:
A heatwave moved slowly north and east across Europe, pushing temperatures to record levels in Germany, Belgium, the Netherlands, and the United Kingdom. Germany's highest temperature ever recorded, 41.3°C, was measured in Saarbrücken. Belgium recorded an unofficial 40°C near Kleine Brogel, and the southern Dutch province of Limburg reached 39.4°C. In the UK, a provisional June record of 37.1°C was set at Cavendish in Suffolk.

Deaths rose in France and Spain. France's health minister named deaths occurring at home as a particular concern, and Paris hospitals were described as "saturated." Public events including a major Paris Pride march and the Solidays music festival were cancelled due to health risks. A Dutch music festival was also shut down after a code red alert was issued across much of the Netherlands.

In Switzerland, both reactors at the Beznau nuclear plant were taken offline after the River Aare reached 25°C — too warm to cool the reactors adequately. Scientists noted this heatwave was the most severe on record for the region.
events.prayer: Residents across Europe, especially the elderly and vulnerable, enduring record-breaking heat with hospitals overwhelmed
events.alert_summary: A heatwave across Europe pushed Germany's all-time record to 41.3°C in Saarbrücken, with at least 150 million people facing temperatures above 35°C.

events.title: Flood in Russia 06/27/2026
events.body:
On June 27, 2026, water levels on the Chikoy and Selenga rivers in Russia's Buryatia region continued to rise. Near the village of Chikkozavod, the Chikoy River overflowed onto the floodplain by 21 centimeters.

In the coming day, officials expect floodwaters to spread further onto the floodplain near the village of Povorot on the Chikoy River, and near Novoselenginsk on the Selenga River.

Residents in these low-lying riverside communities are at risk as water levels continue to climb. No casualty figures were reported in the source.
events.prayer: Villages along the Chikoy and Selenga rivers in Russia facing rising floodwaters
events.alert_summary: Flooding on the Chikoy River spilled 21 cm onto the floodplain near Chikkozavod village, with further rises expected.
